metadata.description: Deterministic medication interaction rules. Each rule is clinician-reviewed and reproducible. Rules fire BEFORE LLM decisions — they are upstream clinical constraints, not downstream corrections.

tier_definitions.A: Hard medical override — MUST change formulation. Auto-executed, no clinician review needed.
tier_definitions.B: Caution adjustment — prefer removal or reduction if alternatives exist. Auto-executed with conditions.
tier_definitions.C: Informational flag — shown for clinician review only. No formulation change.

resolution_types.timing_override: Move components to specific timing (e.g., all to dinner for T4 spacing)
resolution_types.conditional_remove: Remove substance if alternative support already exists in formulation
resolution_types.unconditional_remove: Always remove substance regardless of alternatives
resolution_types.dose_cap: Limit substance to maximum safe dose
resolution_types.flag_only: No auto-action — clinician review flag only

rules.description: Levothyroxine dose is calibrated in 12.5mcg increments. Even modest absorption interference from minerals, fibre, or supplements can shift therapeutic response, cause symptom return, and alter TSH values.

strong_conflicts.mechanism: Chelation and competitive absorption — minerals bind T4 in the gut, reducing bioavailability

moderate_conflicts.mechanism: Fibre adsorbs T4; selenium/iodine affect thyroid metabolism

resolution.reason: Maximum spacing from morning T4 dose. All capsules and prebiotics taken with dinner meal. Consistency is prioritized — client always takes supplements at the same time. Absorption with food remains acceptable.

rules.clinical_note: T4 should be taken on empty stomach, 30-60min before breakfast. All supplements moved to dinner provides maximum spacing (~10-12h). Consistency of evening intake is clinically safer than complex multi-timing schemes.

rules.description: Magnesium bisglycinate has mild blood-pressure-lowering effects. Combined with Ramipril, doses above 400mg may contribute to excessive hypotension. At moderate doses (200-300mg), dizziness or light-headedness may occur.

conflicting_supplements.mechanism: Additive blood-pressure lowering — Mg bisglycinate has mild antihypertensive effect

resolution.reason: Magnesium not necessary when stress/relaxation support is already covered by other components

rules.clinical_note: At doses >400mg Mg + Ramipril, risk of excessive hypotension. At 200-300mg: usually not a major clinical issue but dizziness may occur. For safety: remove magnesium entirely when alternative stress support exists.

rules.description: Curcumin at supplemental doses (500-1000mg/day) is a clinically relevant inhibitor of CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein (P-gp). These are the primary metabolic pathways for many chronic prescription medications. Inhibition can unpredictably raise plasma drug concentrations, increasing risk of toxicity or adverse effects. This rule fires whenever any serious/chronic prescription medication is detected in the client questionnaire.
rules.otc_note: If all client medications match the OTC whitelist, downgrade to Tier C (flag_only). No auto-removal of curcumin.

resolution.reason: CYP3A4/P-gp inhibition risk — curcumin 500-1000mg/day can unpredictably raise plasma concentrations of chronic prescription medications
resolution.replace_note: LLM supplement selector will pick rank 2 or 3 from Anti-inflammatory KB category (Glutathione 50-100mg or Astragalus membranaceus). Selection happens automatically via medication exclusion block in LLM prompt.

rules.clinical_note: Curcumin is a potent CYP3A4/P-gp inhibitor at supplemental doses. For clients on any chronic prescription medication (statins, dopamine agonists, ACE inhibitors, SSRIs, antihypertensives, immunosuppressants, anticoagulants, etc.), the interaction risk is non-trivial and highly variable between individuals. Safe alternatives (Glutathione, Astragalus) provide anti-inflammatory support without CYP3A4 involvement.

known_affected_medications.name: atorvastatin
known_affected_medications.mechanism: Atorvastatin is CYP3A4-metabolized. Curcumin inhibition raises statin plasma concentration — increased myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk.

known_affected_medications.name: cabergoline
known_affected_medications.mechanism: Cabergoline is metabolized via CYP3A4. Curcumin inhibition can raise cabergoline plasma levels unpredictably. Mild dopaminergic pathway interactions also possible.
